channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Petroleum Ministry Confirms Adequate Fuel Stocks in Pakistan Until Mid-June
item.description: The Petroleum Ministry informed Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar that Pakistan has sufficient reserves of petroleum, oil, and lubricants (POL) to last until the third week of June 2026. This assurance ...

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship: Causes, Symptoms and Prevention
item.description: A dangerous hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has claimed three lives and left several others sick, causing worry among health authorities globally. This rare disease is transmitted mainly through ...

item.title: Kenyans Show Growing Trust in Media Despite Ongoing Concerns Over Misinformation
item.description: According to the Media Council of Kenya's 2025 State of the Media Survey, Kenyans are slowly regaining trust in mainstream media. Around 79 percent of the population now reports some level of confiden...

item.title: Sierra Secures $950M to Compete for Leadership in Enterprise AI Market
item.description: Bret Taylor’s AI startup, Sierra, has raised $950 million in a funding round led by Tiger Global and GV, pushing its post-money valuation above $15 billion. The company, which has been rapidly expandi...

item.title: HRCP annual report warns of rising human rights concerns in Pakistan
item.description: The Human Rights Commission of Pakistan (HRCP) released its annual report on the state of human rights in 2025, warning of serious challenges to civic space, judicial independence, and public safety a...

item.title: Meralco Urges Voluntary Participation in Utility Discount Programs
item.description: Meralco, the leading electricity distributor in the Philippines, emphasized that utility discounts, such as those for lifeline rates, senior citizens, and other special programs, should remain volunta...

item.title: Iranian Sailors Repatriated via Pakistan After US Seizure of Ship
item.description: On Monday, fifteen out of twenty-two Iranian sailors who had been evacuated to Pakistan following their release by US forces crossed back into Iranian territory via the Rimdan border in Sistan-Baluchi...

item.title: Ex-Skye Bank Chairman Tunde Ayeni Remanded Over Alleged N8 Billion Fraud
item.description: On Monday, the Federal Capital Territory High Court for Abuja remanded former Skye Bank Chairman, Tunde Ayeni, inside Kuje Correctional Facility as dem await the hearing of him bail application. Ayeni...

item.title: Remembering Vince Rafael: A Life of Love, Care, and Intellectual Brilliance
item.description: This article is a heartfelt eulogy delivered at the memorial service of Prof. Vicente 'Vince' Rafael, a prominent historian from the University of Washington, held in Taguig on May 2, 2026. Vince pass...

item.title: Zimbabwe Court Postpones Sikhala Explosives Case Amid Allegations of Political Targeting
item.description: The legal proceedings against Zimbabwean opposition politician Job Sikhala and his uncle, Alexander Ferrowst Thema, have been postponed to 3 August 2026 by a South African court. The two were apprehen...

item.title: 'Outerplane' Launches Pre-registrations and 3rd Anniversary Updates
item.description: Major9's strategy-based isekai RPG 'Outerplane' is marking its third anniversary with a substantial update and special in-game events. Pre-registrations for the anniversary celebrations are open on th...

item.title: South African Woman Turns Passion for Farming into Successful Livestock Business
item.description: Tlhalefo Dithebe, a young South African woman, made a bold decision in 2021 to leave her stable job in retail business management and pursue her passion for livestock farming. With only R14,000 (rough...

item.title: How Responsible Growth is Shaping Stronger Businesses in Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania is moving into a new phase of economic growth where just having big dreams no longer cuts it. Investors are choosy, customers are expecting more, and communities are watching closely how busi...

item.title: Instant Mobile Loans Rise in Tanzania Amid Low Financial Literacy
item.description: In Tanzania, mobile money loans are quickly changing how people access finance, giving many instant cash for emergencies and daily needs. These digital loans, offered by services like Bustisha by Yas,...

item.title: Congress Retains Bagalkot Assembly Seat with Support from AHINDA Voters and Local Campaign Efforts
item.description: In the recently concluded Bagalkot Assembly by-election, the Congress party successfully retained the seat following the demise of H.Y. Meti. Umesh Meti, son of the late MLA, secured victory aided by ...

item.title: At least 13 injured in Oklahoma lake party shooting, suspects still at large
item.description: On Sunday evening, a mass shooting occurred at a campground near Arcadia Lake in Edmond, Oklahoma, leaving at least 13 people hospitalized. The incident, which took place around 9 p.m., involved two m...

item.title: Heat-related deaths in Karachi as the city hits its hottest day of the year
item.description: At least eight people have died in Karachi due to suspected heatstroke as the city recorded its hottest day of the year on Monday. Rescue officials reported that four bodies were recovered from differ...

item.title: First Ogun Pilgrims Depart from Gateway Airport for 2026 Hajj
item.description: The first batch of pilgrims from Ogun State have successfully arrived in Saudi Arabia for the 2026 Hajj. This marks a historical achievement for Ogun State, as it is the first time pilgrims are depart...

item.title: Minister places NSFAS under administration to stabilise governance and operations
item.description: Minister of Higher Education and Training, Buti Manamela, has officially placed the National Student Financial Aid Scheme (NSFAS) under administration, citing ongoing governance instability, audit fai...

item.title: Tanzania Faces Urgent Need to Adapt to Digital Media and AI
item.description: Back in 2005, Tanzanian journalist Muhidin Issa Michuzi discovered blogging during a conference in Helsinki, Finland, and realised the huge potential of digital media. At that time, most local media h...
